summary: The diff implements all three requirements reasonably well. REQ-1 backend actor exposes upload, retrieve, delete, and getAllImages functions. REQ-2 frontend provides drag-and-drop, file picker, PNG-only validation, live preview, upload button, success state, and delete/replace. REQ-3 applies a clean minimal theme with dashed upload zone, drag-over highlight, and consistent state treatments. One notable concern: the backend does not enforce 'only one thumbnail active at a time' at the actor level — it stores multiple images and relies on the frontend to delete before uploading. Additionally, there are hallucinated features (user profiles, access control/authorization, migration module) not requested in the build spec.

missing_requirements.requirement: Only one thumbnail is active at a time (uploading a new one replaces the previous) — enforced at the backend actor level
missing_requirements.reason: The backend actor stores multiple images in a Map and does not automatically replace the previous thumbnail on upload. The single-active-thumbnail constraint is only enforced client-side in ThumbnailUploadPage.tsx by deleting before uploading, which means the backend itself does not guarantee this invariant.

hallucinated_features.description: User profile management (getCallerUserProfile, getUserProfile, saveCallerUserProfile) added to backend actor
hallucinated_features.reason: No user profile functionality was requested in the build spec. The backend should only expose upload, retrieve, and delete for a thumbnail.

hallucinated_features.description: Access control and authorization system (AccessControl, MixinAuthorization) integrated into the backend
hallucinated_features.reason: The build spec explicitly lists 'User authentication or access control for uploads' as a non-goal. The backend includes role-based permission checks on upload and delete operations.

hallucinated_features.description: Migration module for transforming an old actor state schema
hallucinated_features.reason: No migration from a previous actor schema was requested. The migration.mo file references complex old types unrelated to the thumbnail uploader spec.

hallucinated_features.description: Internet Identity login/logout button in the Header component
hallucinated_features.reason: The build spec lists 'User authentication or access control for uploads' as a non-goal. Adding a login/logout flow via Internet Identity contradicts this.
